executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations victimized in the past 48 hours across all critical sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Peterborough Public Health, Eastern Townships School Board, Municipality of North Perth, and Kids & Company childcare. The Qilin ransomware group leads the attack wave with 20+ victims, followed by Play, SafePay, and a major Clop campaign. The sheer volume and sector diversity—including education, healthcare, municipal government, and financial services—indicates coordinated mass exploitation likely stemming from a common vulnerability or supply chain compromise.

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 20+ Canadian organizations including Service Star Freightways, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, Gradient Wind Engineering, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Eastern Townships School Board, Questica, NovAtel (Hexagon subsidiary), BioPharma Services, CST Coal, Watermark Beach Resort, and multiple professional service firms
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information). Double extortion ransomware with data leak site publication.

items.name: Play
items.targets: 12+ Canadian organizations including Select Tool, Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX Nutrition, Pewarchuk CPA, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter). Known for targeting manufacturing and professional services.

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 11+ Canadian organizations including briwaycarriers.com, mattandsteve.com (agriculture), ripobec.com, groupepiche.ca (construction), precisionaluminum.ca, kenalex.ca, silverlinegroupinc.com, teccart.qc.ca (education), mactavishco.ca
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts). Emerging ransomware variant targeting small-to-medium businesses.

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13+ Canadian organizations including STRATEGICOBJECTIVES.COM, IDEALWELDERS.COM, CMHHELI.COM, SMTLAWYERS.CA (law firm), CLOUDDATAWORKS.CA, SENSIBLESOLUTIONS.CA, TECHNIKEL.CA, MODTECH.CA, EASTPLATS.COM (energy sector), WHISKIJACKRESORTS.COM
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration to Cloud Storage). Known for mass exploitation of file transfer vulnerabilities (MOVEit, GoAnywhere patterns).

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5+ Canadian organizations including Groupe Caddac (construction), ControlGMC (manufacturing), Labtician Ophthalmics (healthcare), Real Tech (manufacturing), Colliers
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021 (Remote Services), T1133 (External Remote Services). Relatively new ransomware-as-a-service operation.

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6+ Canadian organizations including phoenixlabs.com, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros. Construction, soteck.com (agriculture), McKee-Pownall Equine Services, Emond Publishing
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1047 (Windows Management Instrumentation), T1059.001 (PowerShell).

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5+ Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health (critical infrastructure), laurysenkitchens.com, groupecho.com, ktlgroup.com, Trucash (financial services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1204 (User Execution), T1560 (Archive Collected Data). Public health sector targeting indicates potential healthcare focus.

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 8+ Canadian organizations including CPK Interior, excavationtourigny.ca, glasserstv.com, steelworksinc.ca, bisonfamilymedical.com (healthcare), omegatoolcorp.com, reidhurstnagy.com, baisyaakov.ca (education)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562 (Impair Defenses), T1070 (Indicator Removal). Targets small businesses and healthcare.

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations: Active Green + Ross (automotive services), Morison Insurance Brokers (financial services), Kids & Company (childcare services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application). Consumer-facing service targeting.

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Solutions Extreme Technology, Eeyou Communications Network (telecommunications - critical infrastructure)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol). Telecommunications targeting raises critical infrastructure concerns.

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company - database leaked (major Canadian financial services provider)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1078 (Valid Accounts). Confirmed database exfiltration represents significant consumer PII exposure.

items.name: Medusa
items.targets: MRC de Maskinongé (Quebec regional municipality - critical public sector infrastructure)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1210 (Exploitation of Remote Services), T1003 (OS Credential Dumping). Municipal government targeting.

items.name: Worldleaks
items.targets: Municipality of North Perth (Ontario municipal government)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery). Second municipal government victim in this reporting period.

items.name: Akira
items.targets: Inland Audio Visual (business services)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1562.001 (Disable or Modify Tools), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery). Established ransomware group.

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2+ Canadian organizations including Regal Building Materials (construction) and redacted law firm
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter).

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4+ Canadian organizations including beausejourco-op.crs (agriculture), Axion50plus (financial services), ravand.com (technology), and 2 redacted organizations
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery).

items.name: Securotrop
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Living Realty (real estate), Cadman Power Equipment (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1027 (Obfuscated Files or Information).

items.name: CoinbaseCartel
items.targets: Canada Goose (major Canadian consumer brand)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1204 (User Execution). High-profile brand targeting for reputation damage and ransom pressure.

items.name: Bravox
items.targets: John O's Foods (agriculture/food production)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

items.name: Nitrogen
items.targets: Whitfield Welding Inc (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1021.001 (Remote Desktop Protocol).

items.name: Anubis
items.targets: One law firm in Canada (redacted)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact). Legal sector targeting for sensitive client data.

items.name: MedusaLocker
items.targets: dulay.ca (technology)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1489 (Service Stop).

items.name: Payload
items.targets: Q2 Artificial Lift Services (manufacturing)
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact).

canada_landscape.summary: Canada is facing a critical ransomware crisis with 100+ confirmed victims across all provinces and sectors on June 21, 2026. Critical infrastructure has been directly hit: two municipalities (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), public health (Peterborough), telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart college, Bais Yaakov school), and healthcare (multiple medical facilities). No CCCS advisories were published in this 48-hour window despite the unprecedented attack volume.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (apparel retailer), Active Green + Ross (automotive retail), Kids & Company (childcare services) confirmed as victims. No major grocery chains (Loblaws, Sobeys) or department stores (Canadian Tire, SportChek) identified.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered database breach and leak (major payday loan/financial services provider), Morison Insurance Brokers, Trucash (financial services), and multiple accounting/CPA firms (Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant, Reidhurst Nagy) compromised. No major banks (Big Five) directly impacted, but the Money Mart breach exposes significant consumer financial data including loan records and personal information.

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada's cybersecurity agency (CCCS) issued an urgent alert for FortiBleed, a massive leak exposing VPN credentials for 74,000 Fortinet devices globally, impacting 194 countries. Oracle's PeopleSoft zero-day (CVE-2026-35273) is being actively exploited by ransomware gangs and was just added to CISA's known exploited vulnerabilities list. Law enforcement's Operation Endgame cleaned nearly 15,000 WordPress sites infected with SocGholish malware tied to Evil Corp. F5 released emergency patches for critical NGINX vulnerabilities, and 141 Mastra npm packages were backdoored in a supply chain attack targeting developers. DragonForce ransomware is abusing Microsoft Teams to hide command-and-control traffic.

items.description: Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day vulnerability enabling remote code execution
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Joomla Content Editor (JCE) extension unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Critical and high-severity vulnerabilities in NGINX enabling code execution and DoS attacks
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: Cisco ISE/ISE-PIC critical command execution vulnerability allowing authenticated admins to run arbitrary OS commands and escalate to root
items.software_affected: Cisco Identity Services Engine (ISE) and ISE-PIC
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: nv-tlabs GEN3C remote code execution vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to run code via /request-inference endpoint
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

items.description: VMware vCenter denial of service vulnerability
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: None, Integrity: None, Availability: High

items.name: FortiBleed Campaign (Unknown Actor)
items.targets: 74,000 Fortinet devices across 194 countries including government and enterprise networks
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1133 (External Remote Services) - VPN credential theft and exposure

items.name: DragonForce Ransomware
items.targets: US semiconductor manufacturing company
items.ttps: T1071.001 (Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols), T1572 (Protocol Tunneling) - Abusing Microsoft Teams relay systems to hide Backdoor.Turn C2 traffic, file theft, and encryption

items.name: Icarus
items.targets: Klue and other Salesforce customers
items.ttps: T1550.001 (Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token) - OAuth token abuse for Salesforce data theft

items.name: Evil Corp (SocGholish/FakeUpdates)
items.targets: Nearly 15,000 WordPress websites globally
items.ttps: T1189 (Drive-by Compromise), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File) - JavaScript-based malware delivery framework

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Kodak (2.2M records), Council of Europe (429K+ HR/payroll files), Amazon-owned OneMedical.com, Moody Bible Institute
items.ttps: T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service), T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - Mass data exfiltration and extortion

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: Kinetic Education (Australian tutoring provider), Makel Companies Group, Grupo Bimbo
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567.002 (Exfiltration Over Web Service: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage) - Double extortion ransomware attacks

items.name: Mastra npm Supply Chain Attackers
items.targets: Developers using Mastra AI framework - 141 npm packages compromised, 1.1M weekly downloads
items.ttps: T1195.002 (Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - Dormant contributor account takeover, postinstall script payload injection targeting developer credentials and crypto wallets

items.name: ClickFix Campaign Operators (BabaDeda, Lorem Ipsum Loader, Potemkin)
items.targets: General users via fake error messages
items.ttps: T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1566 (Phishing) - AI-generated social engineering delivering SmartRAT, ransomware through modular anti-detection architecture

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: Berg Lilly
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact) - Ransomware encryption and extortion

items.name: Genesis Ransomware
items.targets: United Personnel (Masis Staffing Solutions), Associated Builders and Contractors of Indiana/Kentucky
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Ransomware attacks with data exfiltration

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-014 warning of the FortiBleed campaign exposing thousands of compromised Fortinet VPN credentials affecting devices in 194 countries including Canada. Operation FanTrap uncovered a FIFA 2026 fraud ecosystem targeting the upcoming tournament with matches in Canada. A phishing incident in Newfoundland and Labrador affected the provincial health system (NLHS).
